title: gaultheria howellii
intro: Gaultheria howellii, also known as Howell's Gaultheria, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, where it grows as a low-lying, evergreen shrub. The medicinal properties of Gaultheria howellii are attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid, which is similar in structure to aspirin and has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote cardiovascular health.
